text.title: inc-postmortem-auto-draft-no-publish Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for inc-postmortem-auto-draft-no-publish. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The postmortem agent MUST create the document with status=draft and assignee=incident_commander; MUST NOT possess credentials capable of publishing; MUST mark every non-direct-quote claim as (hypothesis); and MUST refuse to fill the Root Cause field — only Root Cause Hypothesis is agent-writable.
rule.rationale: Auto-publish is the failure mode that destroys the postmortem's purpose: a wrong root cause posted to the org doc becomes the "official" narrative, distorting future decisions. Splitting "Hypothesis" from "Root Cause" preserves the human's authority over the canonical learning.

rule.statement: Every entry in the postmortem Timeline section MUST end with a bracketed citation list referencing at least one signal ID ([slack:T123/p98...], [alert:HighDBLatency@14:02], [deploy:abc123]). The agent MUST refuse to emit timeline lines without a citation, and MUST NOT cite signals outside the supplied closed set.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated timeline entries are the most damaging output of an LLM-drafted postmortem because timestamps look authoritative. Forcing a citation per line makes hallucinations syntactically detectable: a non-existent ID fails a post-draft validator before the human ever reads it.
